params.name: Act of Supremacy
params.description:
On July 1, 1533, Henry VIII prohibited the Church of England from paying tribute to the Roman Church and began drafting the Act of Supremacy.
Under the backdrop of the 16th-century European religious reforms, Henry VIII, citing the Pope's disapproval of his divorce from Queen, began to break away from the Roman Catholic Church. After years of preparation, Henry VIII officially announced the independence of the Church of England from the Roman Church, issuing the Act of Supremacy, designating …
